data.title: Edwards Lifesciences reports Q1 revenue $1.65B, raises 2026 sales and adjusted EPS guidance
data.description:
Edwards Lifesciences reported first-quarter 2026 net sales of $1.6486 billion and net income from continuing operations of $380.7 million. The company posted gross profit of $1.286.0 billion (78.0% margin) and GAAP operating income of $477.6 million, while adjusted diluted EPS was $0.78. Management raised full-year 2026 constant-currency sales growth guidance and increased the midpoint of adjusted EPS guidance after completing a $500 million accelerated share repurchase.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue (Net sales): $1,648.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026 (up from $1,412.7 million in Q1 2025).
  • Gross profit: $1,286.0 million; gross margin 78.0% (GAAP)
  • Operating income (GAAP): $477.6 million; operating margin 29.0% (GAAP)
  • Net income from continuing operations: $380.7 million
  • Adjusted diluted EPS: $0.78 for Q1 2026 (adjusted EPS guidance range for FY2026 raised to $2.95–$3.05)

Business Highlights

  • TAVR momentum: Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement sales were $1,197.3 million in Q1, growing 14.4% year-over-year (11.0% constant currency); company raised full-year TAVR sales growth guidance to 7%–9%.
  • TMTT progress: Transcatheter Mitral and Tricuspid Therapies sales were $175.1 million, driven by adoption of repair and replacement therapies including EVOQUE, PASCAL and newly approved SAPIEN M3 for mitral replacement.
  • Surgical segment: Surgical sales increased to $276.2 million, supported by adoption of RESILIA tissue, INSPIRIS valve uptake and launches of KONECT and expanded MITRIS availability.
  • Clinical and market developments: Positive long-term TAVR trial results (including EARLY TAVR momentum and forthcoming PROGRESS data) and updated European guidelines are shifting care toward earlier, proactive management of aortic stenosis.
  • Capital and access initiatives: Completed $500 million accelerated share repurchase; approximately $1.5 billion remains under share repurchase authorization; CMS is reconsidering the TAVR national coverage determination which could affect access in the U.S.

Original SEC Filing: Edwards Lifesciences Corp [ EW ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Byline Bancorp reports Q1 2026 net income $37.6M; diluted EPS $0.83, NII $99.9M
data.description:
Byline Bancorp reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $37.6 million, or $0.83 diluted earnings per share, up versus the prior quarter. Total revenue for the quarter was $112.4 million, with net interest income of $99.9 million and non-interest income of $12.5 million. The company repurchased nearly $10 million of common stock, raised the quarterly dividend to $0.12 per share, and posted a tangible book value per share of $23.79.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $112.4 million for Q1 2026.
  • Net interest income (NII): $99.863 million for Q1 2026; net interest margin (NIM) 4.33%.
  • Non-interest income: $12.538 million for Q1 2026.
  • Net income: $37.579 million for Q1 2026; diluted earnings per share: $0.83.
  • Non-interest expense: $57.189 million; adjusted efficiency ratio: 49.78%; tangible book value per share: $23.79.

Business Highlights

  • Total assets increased to $9.91 billion as of March 31, 2026, up 2.7% from December 31, 2025.
  • Total deposits grew to $7.802 billion, an increase of $154.4 million quarter-over-quarter, driven principally by time deposits and interest-bearing business checking accounts (including brokered deposits).
  • Total loans and leases held for investment were $7.485 billion; loans sold with government guarantees totaled $71.8 million during the quarter.
  • Asset quality improved: non-performing assets decreased to $70.2 million and net charge-offs declined to $5.95 million for the quarter.
  • Capital deployment and shareholder returns: repurchased 318,208 shares (~$10 million repurchases) and the board declared a cash dividend of $0.12 per share (payable May 19, 2026).

Original SEC Filing: BYLINE BANCORP, INC. [ BY ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Borealis Foods Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On April 17, 2026, the company received a notice from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) because it has not yet filed its 2025 Form 10-K. There is no immediate impact on trading; the company has until June 16, 2026 to submit a compliance plan, may receive up to October 12, 2026 to regain compliance if accepted, and expects to file the 10-K by May 2026.

Original SEC Filing: Borealis Foods Inc. [ BRLS ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: BioMarin clears final French FDI hurdle, merger with Amicus expected to close April 27, 2026
data.description:
BioMarin announced French clearance for its merger with Amicus and expects the deal to close on April 27, 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • French Ministry of Economics and Finance granted foreign direct investment clearance for the merger.
  • Receipt of the French FDI clearance satisfies the final pre-closing condition under the merger agreement.
  • Merger of Merger Sub into Amicus is expected to close on April 27, 2026, with Amicus becoming a wholly owned subsidiary.
  • Forward-looking statements caution that timing and completion remain subject to closing‑time conditions and other risks.

data.title: Ameriprise Financial reports Q1 2026: GAAP EPS $9.68, adjusted operating EPS $11.26; AUA/AUM $1.668T
data.description:
Ameriprise Financial posted strong first-quarter 2026 results, reporting GAAP net income of $915 million and GAAP earnings per diluted share of $9.68. On an adjusted operating basis the company reported $1,064 million of adjusted operating earnings, or adjusted operating diluted EPS of $11.26, up 19% year-over-year. Total assets under management, administration and advisement grew to $1.668 trillion, a 12% increase versus the year-ago quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues (GAAP): $4,886 million for Q1 2026; total net revenues $4,812 million (11% increase vs. Q1 2025).
  • GAAP net income: $915 million; GAAP earnings per diluted share: $9.68 (Q1 2026 vs. $5.83 in Q1 2025).
  • Adjusted operating earnings: $1,064 million; adjusted operating earnings per diluted share: $11.26 (19% increase vs. Q1 2025).
  • Pretax adjusted operating margin: 28.0% consolidated (pretax adjusted operating earnings $1,333 million vs. pretax income $1,144 million reported).
  • Capital returned to shareholders: $936 million in the quarter (dividends paid $152 million; share repurchases $784 million); quarterly dividend increased by 6%.

Business Highlights

  • Assets under management, administration and advisement reached $1.668 trillion, up 12% year-over-year; total assets under management were $1,277 billion and assets under administration $349 billion.
  • Advice & Wealth Management segment: adjusted operating net revenues $3,175 million (up 14%); pretax adjusted operating earnings $951 million with a 30.0% margin; adjusted operating net revenue per advisor (TTM) rose to $1,160k (10% increase).
  • Asset Management segment: assets under management and advisement $706 billion (up 8%); adjusted operating net revenues $910 million and pretax adjusted operating earnings $273 million with a 43.8% net pretax margin; retail and institutional outflows improved versus prior year.
  • Retirement & Protection Solutions: sales increased 10% to $1.3 billion driven by structured variable annuities and variable universal life; pretax adjusted operating earnings $190 million reflecting higher distribution expense and continued variable annuity outflows.
  • Strategic wins and recognition: Huntington National Bank selected Ameriprise as its retail investment program provider (adding ~260 advisors and ~$28 billion in assets); ranked third in JD Power 2026 U.S. Investor Satisfaction (advised segment); Columbia Threadneedle placed in Barron’s top fund families.

Original SEC Filing: AMERIPRISE FINANCIAL INC [ AMP ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Stocks close lower on fading hopes for quick Iran deal, mixed quarterly earnings
data.description:
Key points:
  • Stocks ease on Iran uncertainty
  • Tesla, IBM and Lockheed Martin down after earnings
  • Texas Instruments sees biggest daily jump in more than 25 years
  • Indexes off: Dow 0.36%, S&P 500 0.41%, Nasdaq 0.89%

By Chuck Mikolajczak and Niket Nishant

U.S. stocks fell in choppy trading on Thursday as hopes dimmed for a quick end to the Iran war, while investors grappled with a mixed bag of earnings reports as concerns resurfaced about AI-driven disruption across the software sector.

Equities had been holding near unchanged after Iran tightened control over the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran released footage of its commandos storming a huge cargo ship they claimed to have seized, while demanding the U.S. lift its naval blockade on Iranian ports.

Stocks weakened after reports that Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad ⁠Bagher Ghalibaf had resigned from the negotiating team. Losses were extended as oil prices shot higher after reports of air attacks in Iran.

Iran's Fars news agency said the air defenses were activated due to small drones at several locations across the country.

"We're playing musical chairs between earnings season and these war headlines that are not likely to be that great," said Jay Hatfield, CEO and CIO of Infrastructure Capital Advisors in New York.

"We had a big run, and there are people looking to take some exposure off, and using the war as an excuse is not a bad excuse."

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 179.71 points, or 0.36%, to 49,310.32, the S&P 500 lost 29.50 points, or 0.41%, to 7,108.40 and the Nasdaq Composite lost 219.06 points, or 0.89%, to 24,438.50.

Markets had rallied in recent weeks on hopes a resolution to the Iran war was on the horizon, along with expectations of solid corporate earnings.

But gains have been harder to come by this week. On Monday, the Nasdaq snapped a 13-session streak of gains as optimism faded for a resolution to the war. The three major indexes are slightly lower on the week.

Oil prices holding near $100 a barrel also kept fears of rising inflation in focus.

Data on Thursday showed weekly initial jobless claims increased only marginally last week, but risks from higher prices due to the war could hamper the economy.

S&P Global's flash U.S. Composite PMI Output Index, which tracks the manufacturing and services sectors, increased this month after almost stagnating in March, but the improvement was largely due to what it said was "stock building in the face of concerns over supply availability and price hikes."

PACKED EARNINGS CALENDAR IN FOCUS

The earnings season has been largely strong so far, with 82.1% of the 123 companies that have reported earnings through Thursday morning topping analyst expectations, according to Tajinder Dhillon, head of earnings research at LSEG. The earnings growth rate of 15.6% is up from the 14.4% at the start of the month.

The S&P 500 tech index , down 1.47%, was the worst performing of the 11 major S&P sectors, weighed down in part by an 8.25% drop in IBM after revenue growth slowed in the first quarter on weakness in its software business.

Also weighing on the sector was a 17.75% plunge in ServiceNow after it reported quarterly results and said revenue growth was dented by delays in closing government deals in the Middle East.

The results reawakened concerns that the software sector's traditional business models could be upended by new AI tools, and the S&P 500 software and services index (.SPLRCIS) dropped 5.09% on the session, its biggest daily percentage drop since January 29.

Tesla shares fell 3.56% after the company raised its spending plan to more than $25 billion for the year.

Car-rental company Avis Budget's shares plummeted about 48.38% and recorded their steepest two-day drop ever, after a meteoric rally that was reminiscent of the "meme-stock" craze.

On the flip side, Texas Instruments surged 19.43%, its biggest daily jump since October 2000 after forecasting second-quarter revenue and profit above Wall Street expectations.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.38-to-1 ratio on the NYSE and by a 2.03-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted 41 new 52-week highs and eight new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 124 new highs and 103 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was 17.41 billion shares, compared with the 18.33 billion average for the full session over the last 20 trading days.

data.title: Moody's 10-Q: Revenue $2,079M, EPS $3.73
data.description:
Moody's reported quarterly revenue of $2,079 million and diluted EPS of $3.73, reflecting year-over-year top-line growth and an increase in earnings per share. Revenue rose 8% versus the prior year while diluted EPS increased 8%, supported by operating margin expansion and recurring-revenue strength. Management noted ongoing shifts toward subscription models and some near-term impacts from a reserve for an international non-income tax obligation.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $2,079 million, up 8% versus prior year.
  • Operating Income: $922 million; operating margin 44.3% (modest expansion noted).
  • Net Income: $661 million; net income attributable to Moody's up 6% versus prior year.
  • Diluted EPS: $3.73, diluted EPS attributable to Moody's common shareholders up 8%.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Global revenue growth was broad-based, with both Moody's Analytics (MA) and Moody's Investors Service (MIS) contributing; organic constant-currency growth was +6%.
  • Product & Channel Shift: MA continued migrating customers to cloud subscription models, with ARR increasing 8% and recurring revenue growing significantly, indicating stronger subscription mix.
  • Issuance Demand: MIS benefited from robust issuance-driven demand, led by corporate finance jumbo deals and infrastructure financing activity.
  • Operational Efficiency: Adjusted operating margins expanded in both MA and MIS, aided by cost management measures and restructuring savings.
  • Tax & Compliance Impact: The company recorded a reserve for an international non-income tax obligation that affected near-term operating adjustments.

Original SEC Filing: MOODYS CORP /DE/ [ MCO ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Mobileye Global 10-Q: Revenue $558M, EPS $(4.68)
data.description:
Mobileye Global reported first-quarter revenue of $558 million, up 27% year-over-year, while net loss was $3.818 billion or $(4.68) per share, driven primarily by a $3.788 billion goodwill impairment. Gross profit was $275 million with a 49% margin, modestly improved from the prior year. Results reflect strong EyeQ SoC volume growth alongside one-time non-cash impairment and related tax benefit.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $558 million, up 27% YoY (three months ended March 28, 2026 vs March 29, 2025)
  • Gross Profit: $275 million; gross margin 49% (improved from 47% YoY)
  • Operating Income (loss): $(3,896) million; operating loss largely due to a $3,788 million goodwill impairment
  • Net Income (loss): $(3,818) million, which includes a $64 million tax benefit tied to the goodwill impairment
  • Net Income (loss) per share: $(4.68) basic and diluted; weighted-average shares outstanding 817 million

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Revenue growth was driven by a 28% increase in EyeQ system-on-chip (SoC) volumes and replenishment of safety stock.
  • Product mix: EyeQ SoCs accounted for the majority of revenue (92%), with increasing adoption of higher-priced SuperVision systems contributing to mix improvement.
  • Shipments and scale: Shipped approximately 10.8 million systems in Q1 2026 versus 8.5 million in the prior-year quarter; EyeQ SoCs are deployed in more than 248 million vehicles across about 1,400 models.
  • Strategic expansion: Completed acquisition of Mentee Robotics for $900 million on February 3, 2026 to enter humanoid robotics and the Physical AI market.
  • R&D and product roadmap: Increased R&D investment to support EyeQ6 and next-generation SoCs and to advance Mobileye Surround, SuperVision, Chauffeur, and Drive product rollouts and customer design wins.

Original SEC Filing: Mobileye Global Inc. [ MBLY ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: MaxLinear Q1 10-Q: Revenue $137.2M, GAAP EPS $(0.52)
data.description:
MaxLinear reported first-quarter net revenue of $137.2 million, up 43% year‑over‑year, driven by stronger infrastructure, broadband and industrial demand, while GAAP net loss per share was $(0.52) as tax provisions and other items pushed the company to a net loss. Gross profit improved to $78.9 million with a 58% margin, and operating losses narrowed versus the prior year as operating expenses declined. The firm cited margin improvement from lower intangible amortization and continuing design wins and inventory recovery supporting revenue growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenue: $137.2 million, up $41.3 million (43%) year‑over‑year.
  • Gross profit: $78.9 million; gross margin 58%; up $25.1 million (47%) year‑over‑year.
  • Loss from operations: $(17.2) million, improved from $(46.1) million in the prior period.
  • Net loss: $(45.1) million, with a provision for income taxes of $26.5 million (increase driven by jurisdiction mix and permanent items).
  • Net loss per share: $(0.52) basic and diluted; weighted average shares 87,595 (in thousands).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth led by infrastructure, broadband and industrial end markets, supported by high‑performance analog, optical interconnects, and RF/modem solutions.
  • Geographic and channel mix: Continued heavy shipments through Asia (77% of revenue), with significant sales via Asian ODMs and distributors serving global markets.
  • Product momentum and design wins: New product wins and recovery of customer inventories contributed to demand; ongoing focus on multi‑year design‑in cycles with top‑10 customers representing ~56% of revenue.
  • Operational efficiency and R&D posture: Gross margin benefited from lower intangible amortization; R&D spending was reduced to align with current projects while the company plans future increases to support product roadmaps.

Original SEC Filing: MAXLINEAR, INC [ MXL ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Kinsale Capital Group 10-Q: $466.7M Revenue, $4.88 Diluted EPS
data.description:
Kinsale Capital Group reported quarter revenues of $466.7 million, driven primarily by net earned premiums of $406.9 million and supported by investment and fee income, while diluted earnings per share were $4.88. Net income for the period was $112.554 million, a 26.1% increase year-over-year. The results reflect improved underwriting performance and higher investment income that bolstered operating resources.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $466.7 million (driven by net earned premiums of $406.9 million and investment/fee income)
  • Gross Profit: $139.7 million (reported as Income before income taxes $139.66 million)
  • Operating Income: $112.6 million (reported Net income $112.554 million)
  • Net Income: $112.554 million, up 26.1% year-over-year
  • Diluted EPS: $4.88

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Net earned premiums rose 11.2% year-over-year, fueled by prior-period premium growth and higher net retention on policies.
  • Channel and mix shifts: Broker-driven submissions expanded across most divisions; however, Commercial Property submissions declined 28.3% due to competitive rate pressure.
  • Underwriting performance: Combined ratio improved to 77.4% from 82.1% a year earlier, helped by lower catastrophe losses and favorable development of prior-year loss reserves.
  • Investment and cash flow impact: Higher investment income supported operating results and funded increased purchases of fixed-maturity securities.
  • Reinsurance and retention: Increased retention on reinsurance treaties raised net written premiums and reduced ceded premium levels.

Original SEC Filing: Kinsale Capital Group, Inc. [ KNSL ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Gaming & Leisure Properties 10-Q: $420M real estate income, $0.82 diluted EPS
data.description:
Gaming & Leisure Properties reported total income from real estate of $419.985 million for the quarter, up about $24.8 million year-over-year, and delivered diluted earnings attributable to common shareholders of $0.82 per share versus $0.60 a year earlier. The rise in revenue and EPS was driven by acquisitions, lease escalations and development funding, while operating income and net income benefited from lower operating expenses and a reduced provision for credit losses. Management highlighted continued portfolio growth and significant development and loan funding activity that support future cash rent growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Total income from real estate: $419.985 million (increase of $24.8 million vs prior year)
  • Operating income: $333.349 million (up from $258.834 million prior year)
  • Net income: $239.402 million (up $69.0 million year-over-year)
  • Diluted earnings attributable to common shareholders: $0.82 per share (vs $0.60 prior year)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Increases tied to acquisitions and development funding that added approximately $28.2 million in cash rent, plus contractual lease escalations and variable rent from master leases.
  • Portfolio expansion: Continued acquisitions including assets such as Bally's Lincoln and active development funding commitments (notably Bally's Chicago) to expand the operating portfolio.
  • Brand and tenant momentum: Higher rental contributions from PENN and Bally's master leases supported top-line growth.
  • Development and lending activity: Significant development spending and loan fundings (examples include projects referenced as Ione and Live! Virginia) supporting the company’s pipeline of operating properties.
  • Credit and asset performance: Improved credit outlook reduced the provision for credit losses, reflecting stronger expected real estate values and contributing to lower operating expense recognition.

Original SEC Filing: Gaming & Leisure Properties, Inc. [ GLPI ] - 10-Q - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Kinsale Capital Q1 profit rises
data.description:
Overview

  • US specialty insurer's Q1 net income and operating EPS rose sharply yr/yr

  • Gross written premiums fell 0.5% on lower Commercial Property volumes, offset by growth elsewhere

  • Company repurchased $62.5 mln in shares during Q1

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance or financial forecasts for the current qtr or yr

Result Drivers

  • COMMERCIAL PROPERTY DECLINE - Gross written premiums fell 0.5%, mainly due to a 28.3% drop in the Commercial Property Division from rate decreases and heightened competition

  • HIGHER RETENTION - Net written premiums rose 5.6% due to increased retention on reinsurance treaties effective with the June 2025 renewal

  • UNDERWRITING GAINS - Higher underwriting income was driven by growth in net earned premiums, lower catastrophe losses, and more favorable development of prior-year loss reserves

data.title: Intel Shares Gain as 2Q Forecast Tops Estimates
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Shares of Intel climbed after the chipmaker's second-quarter outlook topped Wall Street's expectations, forecasting an unexpected quarterly profit.

The stock climbed 13% to $75.26 in after-hours trading on Thursday. Through market close, shares were up 81% year to date.

Intel guided for earnings per share of 8 cents, adjusted earnings per share of 20 cents and revenue of $13.8 billion to $14.8 billion for the second quarter. Analysts polled by FactSet had forecast a loss of 6 cents a share, adjusted earnings per share of 10 cents and revenue of $13.1 billion.

The outlook came as the company recorded a wider loss and higher revenue in its latest quarter. Chief Executive Lip-Bu Tan said the results marked a sixth consecutive quarter of revenue above the company's expectations amid increased demand for Intel's CPUs and wafer and advanced packaging offerings.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Financial Institutions Inc Q1 EPS rises 28% on higher interest income
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. regional bank's Q1 net income and EPS rose yr/yr on higher net interest income

  • Net interest margin expanded to 3.67% from 3.35% a year ago

  • Company repurchased 163,197 shares and raised quarterly dividend by 3.2%

Outlook

  • Company continues to target 5% full-year 2026 loan growth

  • Company expects full-year charge-off activity to remain within guided range

  • Company says loan pipelines are healthy and supports confidence in loan growth target

Result Drivers

  • NET INTEREST MARGIN EXPANSION - Margin rose to 3.67% from 3.35% a year ago, driven by lower interest-bearing liability costs

  • COMMERCIAL LENDING - Loan growth was driven by commercial lending in Western and Central New York, though overall loans fell slightly from the prior quarter due to higher payoffs and paydowns

  • EXPENSE MANAGEMENT - Noninterest expenses declined from the prior quarter, reflecting lower incentive compensation and professional services costs

data.title: Orchid Island Capital swings to net loss in Q1 as RMBS losses widen
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. Agency RMBS REIT posted Q1 net loss as market volatility drove realized and unrealized losses

  • Book value per share fell to $7.08 from $7.54 at year-end 2025

  • Net interest income rose, but was offset by RMBS and derivative losses

Outlook

  • Company says return prospects in mortgage market are slightly lower but still attractive

  • Orchid Island Capital views current conditions as conducive for levered Agency RMBS investing

Result Drivers

  • MARKET VOLATILITY - Co said Q1 results were negatively affected by sharp rise in interest rates and mortgage spreads after outbreak of war in Middle East

  • REALIZED AND UNREALIZED LOSSES - Co reported $69.6 mln in net realized and unrealized losses on RMBS and derivatives, offsetting higher net interest income

  • HIGHER PREPAYMENT SPEEDS - Co said prepayment fears mounted and speeds increased dramatically in March, eroding sector performance
